<!-- Generated by the builder-http-route-research-design workflow (run wf_8c7d796e-ba1): 6 OSS projects, 83 issues researched -> synthesized -> adversarially critiqued -> finalized. Source of truth for the Builder route build (slices S1-S14). -->

# conti Builder HTTP Route вЂ” Design (FINAL)

> **Maintainer decisions (2026-06-25), resolving the В§9 open questions that gate the build:**
> - **Prod Builder (Q2): default-OFF, enableable.** `builder.enabled` flag (off in prod by default), FS-writability precheck, 403-loud when disabled. (В§5 / slice S11)
> - **Live rebuild (Q3): INCREMENTAL from day one.** Per-type dispatch (createв†’`registerDetached`, updateв†’`replaceType`/`rebuildType`, dropв†’`removeType`, + relation/inverse targets). The engine already has the primitives; O(change) not O(catalog) вЂ” avoids the R-1 big-data pressure. Slice **S13 is promoted into the core swap (S4)**, not a follow-up.
> - **Legacy cutover (Q5): HARD 410 Gone, immediately.** No translate-and-forward window вЂ” `/content-types/*` and `/component-types/*` mutations return 410 pointing at `/builder/*`, and the meta-write path is deleted. (В§7 / slice S12)
> - **Build progress:** S1вЂ“S6 **DONE**. S1 (mutable `live` cell), S2 (`applySchemaEdit` atomicity), S3 (boot reconciliation guard), S4 (incremental IR build-then-swap + hooks-getter swap-awareness + legacy `/content-types` в†’ 410), S5 (stable-id-keyed `next` + ownership guard + preflight + full route surface), and **S6 (concurrency)**: a per-server single-writer mutex (409+`Retry-After`); an on-disk **content-hash version** (`computeВ­CatalogVersion`) with `If-Match`/412/428 and ETag/304; persisted **`_builder_idempotency`** (adapter-side, post-recompute) with replay + the re-mint guard; `lock_timeout`в†’1500ms + a `55P03`-only bounded retry в†’ 409; `POST /builder/reload` (cache-busted re-import); and the auth-gated 401/403 tests (real better-auth harness in `helpers.ts`). The Builder route is now feature-complete for content-types. Remaining open questions: Q1 (D&P/i18n toggle), Q7 (component-type Builder parity).

The files-first Builder core (`applySchemaEdit`) is wired to nothing. This designs the HTTP route that wraps it, **replaces** the legacy meta path (`content-type.controller.ts` в†’ `content-type.repository.ts` в†’ `rebuildType`), and solves the make-it-LIVE-without-restart problem. One design is recommended throughout; rejected alternatives are noted inline.

---

## 0. Design invariants (carry these through every section)

1. **One source of truth per layer, fused in one operation.** `entities/<apiId>/schema.ts` (files) в†’ `_schema_applied` (snapshot) в†’ live Registry/Engine. The Builder route is the **only writer** of all three, and an apply either advances all three together or none. This is the lesson distilled from every competitor: Strapi/Payload split file-write from boot-DDL; Hasura/PostgREST/Directus split DDL from cache-reload; both splits are the entire bug surface (Strapi #19141, Hasura #8982, supabase #42183, Directus #11436). conti does not split.
2. **Stable ids are the rename key, resolved server-side.** Existing ids come **only** from `_schema_applied` вЂ” never from the client by name. This is the fix for Strapi #12626/#19075/#19141, Directus #17657, Payload #10853, Keystone disc-78. The route must never expose a path that re-mints an existing field's id. **A client-supplied id is only honored if it already exists in the addressed type's snapshot entry** (see В§2 step 3 and В§4 вЂ” closes the read-ids-then-forge-rename vector).
3. **Gate-first, fail-loud, never silent.** `migrateLint` blocks before any write; a blocked edit changes nothing. No silent no-op (Strapi #5038), no permissive client override of the strict default (Hasura #9648).
4. **Single instance в‡’ in-process atomic swap, never NOTIFY/restart.** No ChangeBus/Redis fan-out (Hasura #3720/#1182), no file-watcher-as-apply-mechanism (Strapi #8729/#13947), no fire-and-forget signal (PostgREST #3778/#2791, supabase #42183).
5. **The apply path rebuilds from the in-memory IR, never by re-importing files.** After `migrate()` commits, the served instance is rebuilt from the same `next: ContentTypeSchema[]` IR that lint/diff/reconcile already used (`loadFromSchemas`/`Registry.fromSchemas` take the IR object directly and never read files). ESM re-import (`?v=`) is confined to the explicit out-of-band reload primitive only (В§3.2, В§3.6). This makes the common path **leak-free and drift-free**.

---

## 1. Endpoint shape

### Granularity decision: WHOLE-TYPE PUT (recommended), reject granular-per-field

`applySchemaEdit` is already whole-type: the desired catalog = `_schema_applied` with this `apiId`'s entry replaced by the draft, so **create (apiId new) and update (apiId present) are the same call**. Granular per-field routes (the legacy `/fields`, `/fields/:name`) are *rejected* вЂ” they make rename/reorder a multi-request dance with no atomic boundary and reopen the name-keyed-diff bug class. The SPA already holds the whole-type draft; send it whole.

### Routes (mount under `/builder/*`, retiring `/content-types/*` mutations)

| Method | Path | Purpose | Success | Body |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/builder/content-types` | List applied catalog + `version` (ETag) | 200 | вЂ” |
| `GET` | `/builder/content-types/:apiId` | One applied type (with ids) | 200 / 404 | вЂ” |
| `POST` | `/builder/content-types/:apiId/preview` | Dry-run: mint + lint + codegen, **no write** | 200 | `ContentTypeDraft` |
| `PUT` | `/builder/content-types/:apiId` | Upsert one type в†’ `applySchemaEdit` в†’ live swap | 200 | `ContentTypeDraft` + flags |
| `DELETE` | `/builder/content-types/:apiId` | Drop a type (always destructive) | 200 / 409 | `{ allowDestructive, version }` |
| `POST` | `/builder/reload` | Operator escape hatch: rebuild Registry/Engine from disk (cache-busted re-import) | 200 | вЂ” |

> `:apiId` in the path is the addressed resource; on create the body's `apiId` must equal the path param (else 422). One canonical key, no ambiguity (avoids Directus #25760-style "which object is this").

### Request: `ContentTypeDraft` (in)

The existing `ContentTypeDraft` interface, extended at the HTTP boundary with apply controls:

```jsonc
{
  "apiId": "article",
  "id": "ct_7f3a",                 // OPTIONAL: present=existing(kept), absent=new(minted). Echo back what GET gave you.
  "options": { "draftAndPublish": true, "i18n": false },
  "fields": [
    { "id": "f_91", "name": "title", "cmsType": "string" },   // id present => MUST exist in snapshot; a name change here = lossless rename
    { "name": "subtitle", "cmsType": "string" }               // id absent => new field, id minted server-side
  ],
  "relations": [ /* RelationSchema drafts, ids optional, field/inverse/target validated В§2 */ ],

  // --- apply controls (HTTP-only, NOT part of the stored schema) ---
  "allowDestructive": false,       // explicit ack; default false (В§2)
  "version": "h:9c1fвЂ¦"             // If-Match / optimistic-concurrency token (content hash, В§4). May be an If-Match header instead.
}
```

`allowDestructive` and `version` are stripped before the draft reaches `applySchemaEdit`/codegen вЂ” they are **never** written into `schema.ts`. (Strapi #21753: codegen must not absorb stray keys.)

### Response: uniform envelope (in/out symmetry)

**Always the same shape regardless of outcome** вЂ” the SPA never branches on status code to learn "nothing changed" (Directus #24260's 204-vs-object split is the anti-pattern):

```jsonc
{
  "ok": true,
  "version": "h:af20вЂ¦",           // new catalog version (content hash of on-disk catalog) вЂ” use as next If-Match
  "schema": { /* resolved ContentTypeSchema WITH minted ids вЂ” feed straight back into the next PUT */ },
  "applied": [ /* readonly Change[] that hit the DB; [] on no-op */ ],
  "blocked": [],                  // populated only on ok:false
  "live": true                    // the new Registry/Engine is serving (false would never return 200; see В§3)
}
```

> **SPA contract (load-bearing for idempotency, В§4):** a retry MUST resend the `schema` from this envelope (ids present), never the original id-less body. The server additionally guards this server-side (В§2 step 3, В§4).

`SchemaEditResult` already returns `{ ok, blocked?, applied?, schema? }`; the route adds `version` + `live` + always-present `applied:[]`/`blocked:[]`.

### Status codes

| Situation | Status | Body |
|---|---|---|
| Applied (incl. no-op `applied:[]`) and live | **200** | envelope `ok:true` |
| Preview (dry-run) | **200** | `{ ok, blocked, applied:[вЂ¦proposed], generatedSource, version }` |
| Invalid body / bad identifier / apiIdв‰ path / unknown cmsType / dangling relation target / client id not in snapshot / duplicate id in draft | **422** | `{ ok:false, error, detail }` |
| Reserved name / apiId collision | **409** | `{ ok:false, error }` |
| Destructive op without `allowDestructive` | **409** | `{ ok:false, blocked:[вЂ¦], error:"requires allowDestructive" }` |
| Optimistic-concurrency mismatch (stale `version`) | **412** | `{ ok:false, error, currentVersion }` |
| Another edit in flight (Builder lock held) | **409** | `{ ok:false, error:"schema edit in progress; retry" }` (with `Retry-After`) |
| Lock-timeout on a busy table (ACCESS EXCLUSIVE wait) | **409** | `{ ok:false, error:"schema lock timed out; retry" }` |
| `MigrationDataLossError` (lossy shrink would truncate rows) | **422** | `{ ok:false, error, table, column, affected }` |
| `MigrationUnsupportedError` (D&P/i18n toggle on existing type) | **422** | `{ ok:false, error }` (until В§9 resolves) |
| Builder disabled in this env | **403** | `{ ok:false, error:"builder disabled" }` (never silent вЂ” Strapi #5038) |
| Live rebuild threw (DB+file committed, swap failed) | **500** | `{ ok:false, error:"schema applied but reload failed; last-good still serving" }` (В§3) |

---

## 2. Apply flow

```
PUT /builder/content-types/:apiId
  в”‚
  в”њв”Ђ 0. ENV GATE        builder enabled here? (В§5)            в†’ 403 if off (loud)
  в”њв”Ђ 1. AUTH GATE       gate(res, req, 'builder.manage', вЂ¦)   в†’ 401/403 (В§6)
  в”њв”Ђ 2. ACQUIRE BUILDER LOCK (single-writer mutex, В§4)        в†’ 409 if held
  в”‚       *** the lock is taken BEFORE the first catalog read and held through swap ***
  в”‚       (covers read-catalog в†’ lint в†’ write-temp в†’ migrate в†’ swap as ONE critical section)
  в”њв”Ђ 3. READ CATALOG ONCE + PARSE + VALIDATE
  в”‚       - read the applied catalog ONCE here (used for version check, lint, id-ownership) вЂ” В§4
  в”‚       - body is object; body.apiId === :apiId             в†’ 422
  в”‚       - EVERY emitted identifier matches /^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$/, not a JS reserved
  в”‚         word, not a RESERVED_FIELD_NAME (id/document_id/created_at/updated_at/
  в”‚         published_at/locale): apiId, every field.name, AND every relation.field +
  в”‚         relation.inverse                                  в†’ 409/422
  в”‚         *** SECURITY: names are emitted as RAW object keys by codegen;
  в”‚             an unvalidated name is arbitrary-code injection into schema.ts ***
  в”‚       - existence (HARD 422, not a warning): every relation.target resolves to an
  в”‚         existing apiId; every component/components ref exists. A dangling target makes
  в”‚         Registry.fromSchemas/buildEngine THROW at swap time в†’ 500, so reject up front.
  в”‚       - enum value strings: reject control chars (emitted as source via lit()).
  в”‚       - ID OWNERSHIP (В§0.2, В§4): every client-supplied field id MUST already exist in
  в”‚         the addressed type's snapshot entry; ids MUST be unique within the draft; a NEW
  в”‚         field MUST NOT carry an id belonging to a different existing field в†’ 422.
  в”њв”Ђ 4. OPTIMISTIC CONCURRENCY  body.version === currentVersion (on-disk content hash)?  в†’ 412 (В§4)
  в”њв”Ђ 5. applySchemaEdit(sql, entitiesDir, draft, { allowDestructive })  вЂ” HARDENED В§2.1
  в”‚       a. mintMissingIds   (existing ids preserved вЂ” rename safety)
  в”‚       b. migrateLint  в”Ђв”Ђ blocked? в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв–є 409, NOTHING written
  в”‚       c. write generated source to TEMP file (NOT schema.ts yet, В§2.1)
  в”‚       d. migrate()  в”Ђв”Ђ ONE tx: advisory lock + DDL + reconcile _schema_applied
  в”‚            в”њв”Ђ MigrationBlockedError      в†’ 409
  в”‚            в”њв”Ђ MigrationDataLossError     в†’ 422
  в”‚            в”њв”Ђ MigrationUnsupportedError  в†’ 422
  в”‚            в””в”Ђ any throw в†’ tx ROLLS BACK; unlink temp, leave schema.ts untouched; re-raise
  в”‚       e. on commit в†’ fs.rename(temp, schema.ts)  (atomic flip, В§2.1)
  в”њв”Ђ 6. MAKE IT LIVE   build-new FROM THE `next` IR в†’ validate в†’ atomic swap (В§3) вЂ” NO re-import
  в”‚       в””в”Ђ rebuild throws в†’ KEEP last-good serving, return 500 (loud)
  в”њв”Ђ 7. RELEASE BUILDER LOCK (finally)
  в””в”Ђ 8. return 200 envelope  (ok only AFTER swap is serving)
```

### 2.1 Hardening `applySchemaEdit` вЂ” close the file/DB ordering gap

The current core (`builder.ts:77`) writes `schema.ts` **before** `migrate()` (line 78) and **outside** the DB tx. A `migrate()` failure leaves the file ahead of the DB вЂ” exactly the drift conti exists to prevent (the disk-says-renamed/DB-says-old split that mirrors Hasura #8982). **Fix:**

- Write generated source to a **temp file** in the type's dir.
- Run `migrate()`.
- On commit success в†’ `fs.rename(tmp, schema.ts)` (atomic on same fs).
- On `migrate()` throw в†’ unlink the temp, leave the existing `schema.ts` untouched, re-raise.

This makes the file flip part of the all-or-nothing unit without putting fs I/O inside the pg tx.

**Residual crash window + the boot guard (this is the part the invariant in В§3.4 actually depends on).** If the process crashes *between* `migrate()` COMMIT and `fs.rename`, the DB is ahead of the file (file behind). The window is sub-millisecond, but "HALT LOUD" cannot be mere prose вЂ” it must be **implemented at boot**:

- On boot, compute `diff(files, _schema_applied)`.
- If the diff is empty в†’ normal boot.
- If the diff contains **only forward, non-destructive changes** в†’ files are genuinely ahead (a legitimate out-of-band edit); proceed (seed/migrate).
- If the diff contains **any DESTRUCTIVE reverse change** (a drop/retype that would undo something `_schema_applied` records as already applied) в†’ this is the crash-window signature (**files behind snapshot**). **Do NOT let `migrate()` apply the reverse drop.** Instead **either HALT LOUD** (surface via `GET /builder` + logs) **or recover the file forward from `_schema_applied.schema`** (the canonical JSON IR, В§3.5) and continue. The chosen default is **recover-forward** (snapshot is the post-commit truth for that window), logging the recovery; HALT is the fallback if the file cannot be safely regenerated.

This encodes the direction explicitly: **file behind в‡’ recover-forward from snapshot; file ahead в‡’ apply forward.** Without this guard the "files drive, snapshot is bookkeeping; disagree в‡’ HALT" invariant is unbacked.

### 2.2 The three migrate error classes в†’ HTTP

| Error class | HTTP | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `MigrationBlockedError` | **409 Conflict** | A precondition (the ack) is unmet; resubmit with `allowDestructive:true` after the operator confirms the `blocked[]` list (surfaced verbatim from `describeChange`). |
| `MigrationDataLossError` | **422 Unprocessable Entity** | The request *is* acked but real rows would truncate/round; the data, not the request form, makes it unprocessable. Echo `{table, column, affected}` so the SPA tells the operator to widen/clean first. **Not** overridable by `allowDestructive` вЂ” fail loud (Strapi #19141's 68k-row lesson). |
| `MigrationUnsupportedError` | **422** | D&P/i18n toggle on an existing type is deferred in `migrate` today; reject loudly, never silently skip (В§9 + Strapi #19141 treats toggles as a rebuild вЂ” conti must treat them as a tested data migration, not drop one). |

### 2.3 `allowDestructive` as an explicit request flag

A boolean in the body (default `false`), threaded straight to `applySchemaEdit({ allowDestructive })`. **Never auto-true.** The intended UX: the SPA first calls `POST вЂ¦/preview` (no write), shows the operator the exact `blocked[]` destructive ops + generated source, the operator confirms, then the SPA re-PUTs with `allowDestructive:true`. This is the per-op ack the migrate engine already models, surfaced honestly (vs Skeema #51 / Drizzle #3209 blanket `--force`). `DELETE` (always a DROP TABLE) requires `allowDestructive:true` too.

---

## 3. The hard part вЂ” live in-process reload

**Goal:** after `migrate()` commits, the rewritten `schema.ts` + migrated DB become the served Registry/Engine **synchronously, in the same call, with zero restart**, or the apply fails loud while the **old** instance keeps serving.

### 3.1 The const-capture problem (must fix first)

Today `uws.adapter.ts` does `const current = engine;` (line 377) and **every route closure reads `current`**. A reload cannot reassign a `const`. Introduce a **mutable holder** captured once:

```ts
// in createServer
const live = { engine, registry, hooks };          // single mutable cell
// routes read live.engine / live.registry / live.hooks (replacing `current`)
```

The atomic swap is then a single synchronous assignment `live.engine = newEngine; live.registry = newReg; live.hooks = newHooks;` вЂ” JS is single-threaded, so no in-flight request observes a half-swapped cell. This holder is exposed to the Builder route via the context so it can perform the swap. **Every long-lived subsystem that reads schema reads it through `live`** вЂ” this is the Directus #25500 fix (auth/RBAC must not freeze a private schema snapshot). RBAC/auth here key on *roles/actions* (`builder.manage`), not on per-field schema, so they need no rebind; but the rule is stated so a future field-level check can't regress.

### 3.2 Apply path rebuilds from the in-memory IR вЂ” no ESM re-import

`store.loadFromSchemas(schemas)` в†’ `Registry.fromSchemas(schemas)` (postgres.store.ts:58-62, registry.ts:696) takes the **IR object directly and never reads files**. `migrate()` already holds `next: ContentTypeSchema[]` вЂ” the full reconciled catalog it just linted, diffed, and wrote into `_schema_applied`. Therefore the apply path rebuilds the served instance **entirely from that in-memory `next` IR**:

- **Zero ESM re-import**, zero `?v=` query string, **zero module-graph leak** on the common path.
- **Zero re-introspect drift** вЂ” the rebuilt instance is built from the exact object `migrate()` reconciled, so it cannot disagree with the DB it just wrote.

The entire ESM-leak / reload-counter / "recommend restart for edit-loops" apparatus that an earlier draft attached to the apply path is **dropped** вЂ” the architecture does not have that problem here. ESM re-import survives **only** in `POST /builder/reload` (В§3.6), where files genuinely changed out-of-band and the IR must be re-derived from disk; that confines the unavoidable module-graph leak (node #38322, aral-esm-leak) to one explicit, infrequent operator primitive.

### 3.3 Build-then-atomic-swap (never mutate live in place)

```
1. take the `next` IR migrate() just reconciled (NO file re-import on the apply path)
2. build a NEW Engine+Registry off to the side via loadFromSchemas(next)/Registry.fromSchemas(next)
     вЂ” re-stream rows from the already-migrated DB
     вЂ” this is where a broken IR / dropped-ref would throw (but В§2 step 3 made dangling refs a 422 already)
3. validate the new instance fully (it has the new type + new field, ids intact)
4. ATOMIC SWAP:  live.engine = new; live.registry = new; live.hooks = new
5. dispose: drop the old instance's references (GC); arenas freed
   on ANY throw in 1вЂ“3: discard the new instance, KEEP live.* pointing at old, re-raise в†’ 500
```

This is the PostgREST/Hasura schema-cache-rebuild model (PR #1574) **minus** the cross-process NOTIFY вЂ” a direct in-process pointer swap. It is **synchronous with apply**: the PUT returns 200 only after step 4, so the SPA never sees the supabase #42183 / PGRST204 window where the DDL is done but the engine still rejects the new field.

**v1 = full-catalog side-build + atomic pointer swap. This handles create / update / drop uniformly** and is required because `engine.replaceType` (engine.ts:480-481) **throws if the name is not already defined**, and `registerDetached` (457-458) throws if it **is** вЂ” so `replaceType` alone cannot create or drop a type. Building a whole new Engine off to the side and swapping the `live` pointer sidesteps that entirely: a create, an update, and a drop are all just "the new catalog differs from the old," materialized once.

**Incremental rebuild is an explicit, fully-specified follow-up (not vague):** when it lands it must **dispatch by change kind**, not call `replaceType` blindly:

- **new type** в†’ `buildDetached` + `registerDetached` + add to the registry,
- **changed type** в†’ `replaceType` / `rebuildType` (re-streams that one type),
- **dropped type** в†’ `removeType` (engine.ts ~494),
- **plus rebuild the relation/inverse targets** of any touched type (as `rebuildType`/`syncRelation` already re-derive relations).

This is O(change), reuses tested per-type code, and avoids re-streaming untouched types. See В§4 (lock pressure) and Risks (R-1) for why this is strongly preferred and the threshold under which the v1 full rebuild is acceptable.

**Do NOT re-run DDL during reload** вЂ” reload reads the already-migrated DB (Strapi #19141/#13428 died precisely because the destructive op fired at restart-rebuild time; conti's reload must never "recreate" tables).

### 3.4 Three-way consistency & ordering

The ordering is the consistency guarantee:

```
DB tx commits  в”Ђв–є  file flips (tempв†’real)  в”Ђв–є  in-process swap  в”Ђв–є  200
   (truth)            (source)                   (served)
```

- Crash **after DB commit, before file flip**: file is behind DB. **Recovery is implemented (В§2.1 boot guard):** boot detects "destructive reverse diff" = files-behind and **recovers forward from `_schema_applied.schema`** (or HALTs LOUD) вЂ” it never lets `migrate()` apply the reverse drop. The temp-write makes the file flip near-atomic and *last* before swap, so this window is sub-millisecond.
- Crash **after file flip, before swap**: DB+file agree, only the in-RAM instance is stale вЂ” and the process is dead anyway. Next boot rebuilds from disk truth. This is why **boot always rebuilds from disk, never trusts a persisted cache** (PostgREST recovers on listener-reconnect precisely because signals get lost).

The authoritative rule: **files drive, `_schema_applied` is bookkeeping** вЂ” and the one window where they can disagree (file behind) is handled by the В§2.1 boot guard, not by prose.

### 3.5 Safe-boot / last-good fallback

If a rewritten `schema.ts` fails to import or the Registry fails to rebuild **at process boot**, do **not** crash the process (Hasura #6344/#3381 catch-22: the fix-it route dies with the server). Fall back to building the Registry from the last-good `_schema_applied` snapshot and stay up, surfacing the error via `GET /builder` + logs, so `POST /builder/reload` remains reachable to push a corrected file. (`_schema_applied.schema` is the canonical JSON IR вЂ” sufficient to rebuild without the files, and the recovery source for the В§2.1 boot guard.)

### 3.6 `POST /builder/reload` (operator primitive) вЂ” the ONLY re-import path

Explicit, idempotent "rebuild Registry/Engine from disk" вЂ” the Hasura #292 reload primitive. Used after an out-of-band file edit (git pull in dev), or to recover after a safe-boot fallback. Because the files genuinely changed, the IR must be **re-derived from disk**, so this path **does** cache-bust the ESM import:

- `loadTypes()` must accept a **cache-bust token** and append `?v=<token>` to **every** imported `schema.ts` **and** `hooks.ts` (`load.ts:55/62` import via `pathToFileURL(...).href` with NO query today, and the file explicitly assumes "module caching is harmless: boot loads once"). Without the token the cached (OLD) module graph is returned and the reload is a **silent stale no-op** вЂ” exactly the Strapi #5038 class.
- The token MUST change per reload (file mtime or a monotonic counter); a stable token re-hits the cache.
- **Documented limitation:** `?v=` re-imports `schema.ts`/`hooks.ts` themselves, but **transitively imported modules** (shared helpers, `@conti/core`) are **not** re-imported unless they too carry the query. So `POST /builder/reload` reliably refreshes only `schema.ts`/`hooks.ts`; a helper-file edit is missed. **A full process restart remains the only fully-correct refresh of the transitive module graph** вЂ” state this in operator docs.
- Same build-then-swap as В§3.3; **never re-runs DDL**.
- Reload **also bumps the catalog version** (В§4) so a subsequent PUT carrying a pre-reload version is correctly rejected 412.

---

## 4. Concurrency

### Single-writer Builder lock (in-process async mutex) вЂ” the primary guard, held from the FIRST catalog read

Today the advisory lock is taken **inside** `migrate()` (`migrate.ts:364`, `pg_advisory_xact_lock` within `sql.begin`), *after* `applySchemaEdit` has already read the catalog (`builder.ts:67`), linted (71), and written the file (77). Two concurrent PUTs can both pass `migrateLint` and both `writeFile` (last-writer-wins on `schema.ts`) before either reaches the DB lock вЂ” the file/DB interleave. **Fix:** an **app-level async mutex** acquired at flow **step 2, before the first catalog read**, covering the **entire** read-catalog в†’ validate в†’ lint в†’ write-temp в†’ migrate в†’ swap sequence.

- Single instance в‡’ an in-process mutex is sufficient and is the correct serialization boundary (Atlas-style single-writer). The migrate advisory lock stays as **defense-in-depth** against a stray second instance / the dev watcher.
- **Collapse the double catalog read.** Today the route would read `_schema_applied` once (for version + lint) and `migrate()` re-reads it internally (`migrate.ts:394` via `ensureAppliedTable`/`readAppliedSchemas`) вЂ” two reads with the file-write between them. Read the applied catalog **once** under the lock and pass `next` straight through; `migrate()`'s internal re-read is then redundant work under the same held lock (harmless, but noted вЂ” a future cleanup can pass the already-read catalog in). The mutex guarantees the lint-read, the file write, and any migrate-internal read all see one consistent snapshot.
- A second concurrent PUT gets **409 + Retry-After**, not a queue (queueing risks unbounded latency behind a slow ALTER).

### Optimistic concurrency (version / ETag) вЂ” derived from ON-DISK content, not the snapshot

The catalog read-replace-write in `applySchemaEdit` is a classic lost-update race when two admins edit different fields of the same type. Add a **catalog version**, but it MUST track the thing that is the source of truth вЂ” the **files**, not `_schema_applied`. A snapshot-derived token (`max(applied_at)+count`) would **not advance** when an operator edits `schema.ts` on disk and runs `POST /builder/reload` (reload never migrates), so a later PUT carrying the old version would pass the 412 check while the on-disk source has diverged вЂ” a stale edit slipping through in exactly the dev-watcher / out-of-band scenario this is meant to catch.

- **Version = a content hash of the on-disk catalog** (open question #4's hash option). Robust against clock issues; it is what actually moves when files move. Recomputed on every `GET`, on apply (post-flip), **and on `POST /builder/reload`** (В§3.6).
- `GET` returns it; `PUT`/`DELETE` require `If-Match` (or `body.version`); mismatch в‡’ **412 + currentVersion**. Default to **refusing** a stale apply (Directus #24538: provide no blind-force; the operator re-fetches and resubmits).

### Idempotency key + server-side re-mint guard вЂ” both warranted

A retried PUT after a *committed-but-connection-dropped* apply must **not** re-run `mintMissingIds` (`builder.ts:44-52` mints for any field whose `id` is **absent**) and mint fresh ids for already-created fields в†’ turn the prior create into drop+add = Strapi #12626 data loss. Server-side id resolution only **partially** protects: a naive retry of the **original** body (ids still absent) re-mints. So the key is **load-bearing, not optional**, for the create-time window. Two layers:

1. **`Idempotency-Key` header, persisted (not in-memory).** On a single instance, in-memory storage is lost on the exact event the key protects against вЂ” a crash/restart mid-retry. Store first-result in a **`_builder_idempotency` table** keyed by the key, holding the response envelope + a TTL; return the stored envelope on replay (Stripe model). Survives restart.
2. **Server-side re-mint guard (defense-in-depth, even without the key).** Per В§2 step 3: reject (422) a PUT whose `version` matches an existing type but whose `fields` lack ids that the snapshot already has for that type. This catches the re-mint footgun structurally, not only via the optional header. Combined with the SPA contract (resend the `schema` envelope, ids present), the create-time window is closed definitively.

### Postgres lock pressure

`migrate()` sets `SET LOCAL lock_timeout='5s'`. An ALTER takes ACCESS EXCLUSIVE; on a busy table it queues every conflicting query behind it (xata lock-queue, gh-ost cutover). **Recommend lowering toward `<2s`** and adding **bounded retry-with-backoff** on `lock_timeout` failures, surfaced as 409 (not a hang). Run schema DDL on a **dedicated short-lived `max:1` connection** (the repo already documents this requirement) so the read/write pool isn't starved (PostgREST #3020). `CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY` is forbidden in the one-tx model (Directus #26697) вЂ” out of scope until conti adds user indexes; note it as a future carve-out.

---

## 5. Dev vs prod gating

**Recommendation: Builder ON in prod, but gated вЂ” opposite of Strapi's blanket ban.**

Strapi/Payload hard-disable prod schema edits (Strapi #4798/#5038) because their model = write-files-then-**restart**, which doesn't compose with multi-instance + read-only prod FS. **conti is single-instance by mandate and applies in-process with atomic swap** вЂ” the three justifications for the ban don't hold:

- No fleet to desync (Hasura #3720 is N/A).
- No restart, so no "restart taking longer than expected" (Strapi #13947).
- We own the FS-writability + atomic-apply story they punted on.

So conti *can* allow a guarded prod Builder. **But gate it explicitly:**

1. **Config flag** `builder.enabled` (default: `true` in dev, **`false` in prod unless explicitly enabled**). Disabled в‡’ **403, loud** (never the silent no-op of Strapi #5038).
2. **FS-writability precheck** at apply: confirm `entities/` is writable (a read-only prod FS, immutable container) в‡’ 403 with reason **before** touching the DB (Hasura #6913: validate external refs at validate-time, not crash mid-apply).
3. **No apply-path ESM leak to worry about** (В§3.2 rebuilds from the IR). The leak is confined to `POST /builder/reload`; in prod, reload is the rare operator primitive, and a restart is the documented fully-correct refresh anyway (В§3.6).

**Why not OFF-in-prod like Strapi:** the headline differentiator is a guided, lossless, zero-downtime schema change вЂ” disabling it in prod throws that away and forces the edit-in-devв†’commitв†’redeploy dance (which re-runs DDL on prod data, the *same* risk, just later вЂ” Strapi #19141 on prod data). Default-off-but-enableable respects the cautious operator while keeping the capability. *(Rejected alternative: always-on with no flag вЂ” too sharp an edge for a remote code-write + DDL surface.)*

---

## 6. Auth / RBAC

Reuse the existing be-09b primitives exactly as the legacy routes already do: `gate(res, req, 'builder.manage', true, вЂ¦)` fronts **every** mutating Builder route (`PUT`, `DELETE`, `POST вЂ¦/preview`, `POST /builder/reload`). `gate` resolves the principal via `SessionCache.validate` (off-heap, zero-PG warm) or an API key, applies the **401 (unauthenticated) vs 403 (authenticated-but-unauthorized)** split, and buffers the body. `GET /builder/*` stays public-by-config like the read routes.

This is the **highest-privilege endpoint in the system** вЂ” it writes executable `.ts` and runs DDL. `builder.manage` should be admin-only and is a coarse, type-agnostic action (no field/row-level RBAC вЂ” dropped as YAGNI per auth design). Combined with В§1/В§2's identifier validation, an injection payload in `apiId`/field name is rejected 422 with nothing written.

**Public GET exposes stable ids вЂ” confirmed non-secret, but the PUT path is what enforces the security model.** Ids are non-secret for confidentiality, but they are the rename key (В§0.2), so the **whole** model depends on the PUT path never trusting a client-supplied id to **match** an existing field unless it is present in `_schema_applied`. An attacker who reads ids via public `GET` could otherwise craft a body that re-targets an existing field's id onto a different field (an id-collision rename) вЂ” `mintMissingIds` (`builder.ts:48`) preserves any client-supplied id verbatim with no existence/uniqueness check. **В§2 step 3 closes this:** every client-supplied id MUST already exist in the addressed type's snapshot entry; ids MUST be unique within the draft; a new field carrying an id that belongs to a different existing field is rejected 422. This is a hard server-side check, independent of who can read the ids.

---

## 7. Legacy cutover

The legacy chain is: `/content-types/*` mutations в†’ `handleContentTypeRequest` в†’ `content-type.repository.ts` (`createContentType`/`addField`/`renameField`/`changeFieldType`/`dropField`/`dropContentType`/`addRelation`) в†’ `content_types`/`content_type_fields`/`content_type_relations` **meta tables** в†’ per-type `syncCreate`/`syncSchema`/`syncDrop` RAM sync. This is the pre-pivot design the files-first pivot replaces.

### Cutover steps

1. **Land `/builder/*`** (whole-type PUT/DELETE/preview/reload) wired to the hardened `applySchemaEdit` + the В§3 swap, gated identically (`builder.manage`).
2. **Redirect or 410 the legacy mutations.** `POST/PUT/DELETE /content-types/*` and `/fields/*`, `/relations` в†’ either (a) translate to a whole-type draft and forward to the new path (kindest for any existing SPA), or (b) return **410 Gone** with a pointer to `/builder/*`. Recommend **(a) during a deprecation window, then (b)**.
3. **Keep `GET /content-types/*` and `/component-types` reads** вЂ” the admin/SDK read `projectDef` from them. Back the `GET` with the new applied catalog (or keep registry-projected; they agree once the swap is the writer).
4. **Migrate component-types the same way** (`/component-types/*` в†’ a component-type slice of `applySchemaEdit`; out of this doc's core scope but identical shape).

### What becomes deletable after cutover

Once **no writer touches the meta tables**:

- **`content-type.repository.ts`** вЂ” `createContentType`, `addField`, `renameField`, `changeFieldType`, `dropField`, `dropContentType`, `addRelation` (the meta-write ops). Keep the **pure helpers** still used by the files-first path: `resolveFields` and `resolveType`/`classifyTypeChange` are imported by `migrate.ts`/`adapt.ts` вЂ” those stay (move to a `type.catalog`/`resolve` module so the deletion is clean).
- **`Registry.rebuildType`** (the meta-re-query rebuild) вЂ” replaced by the В§3 build-from-IR rebuild. Note: the **data-write path** in `uws.adapter.ts` (`ctx.rebuild` в†’ `rebuildType(store.sql, current, def, registry)` in `engine.loader.ts`) re-streams rows for a *data* write and does **not** re-query meta вЂ” that stays. Only the **meta-driven schema rebuild** (`registry.rebuildType` reading `content_types`) is retired. (The per-type `replaceType`/`rebuildType`/`removeType` primitives are **retained** вЂ” they are the building blocks for the В§3.3 incremental follow-up.)
- **The meta tables themselves** вЂ” `content_types`, `content_type_fields`, `content_type_relations` (and the component equivalents) can be **dropped from the init migration** (migration policy: ONE consolidated init, drop & recreate pre-launch вЂ” no backfill needed). `RESERVED_TABLE_NAMES` keeps reserving the names defensively.
- **`handleContentTypeRequest`'s granular branches** (`/fields`, `/fields/:name` PUT/DELETE, `/relations`) and `syncCreate`/`syncSchema`/`syncRelation`/`syncDrop` вЂ” superseded by the whole-type swap.

**Keep:** `ddl.ts` (compile-only builders, reserved names, identifier validation вЂ” reused by `migrate.ts`), `type.catalog.ts`, the typed-error classes (re-mapped to the new status table), the per-type engine primitives for incremental rebuild.

---

## 8. Pitfalls-avoided table

| Competitor issue (project #) | Failure | conti's decision that avoids it |
|---|---|---|
| Strapi #12626 / #19075 | Rename = drop+add column в†’ data erased | Stable minted field ids from `_schema_applied`; rename = `ALTER RENAME` (`planRenameSteps`). Ids resolved server-side, never client-by-name; client ids honored only if present in snapshot (В§0.2, В§2, В§4, В§6). |
| Strapi #19141 | D&P-off + add field в†’ 68k rows dropped on restart | `migrateLint` gates destructive ops; toggles are `MigrationUnsupportedError`в†’422 until modeled as a tested migration (В§2.2, В§9); reload never re-runs DDL (В§3.3). |
| Strapi #13428 | Enum edit crashed, wiped data on restart | Enum = CHECK swap inside the ONE migrate tx; rollback atomic (`applyOne` enum path). Reload doesn't recreate tables. |
| Strapi #21753 | Wholesale file rewrite clobbers hand-authored keys | `generateSchemaSource` round-trips 100% of the DSL; `hooks.ts` is a separate file the Builder never rewrites; apply-controls stripped before codegen (В§0.1, В§1, CI round-trip assertion В§9). |
| Strapi #4798 / #5038 | Prod schema edits banned / silently no-op | Single-instance в‡’ guarded prod Builder allowed; disabled state returns **403 loud**, never silent; reload cache-busts so it is never a silent stale no-op (В§5, В§3.6). |
| Strapi #13947 / #14611 / #8729 | Restart loops, "taking longer than expected", watcher-coupled apply | Apply is HTTP-driven (`applySchemaEdit`) + in-process atomic swap from the IR; **no restart**, no watcher-as-apply (В§3, В§0.4). |
| Strapi #20551 | Lifecycle hooks stop firing after regen | `hooks.ts` separate; re-imported and swapped in the same `live` cell as the engine (В§3.1, В§3.6). |
| Strapi #23736 | Editing apiId bricks the type (invalidUID) | apiId decoupled from stable id в‡’ apiId change is a safe `ALTER RENAME`; uniqueness/reserved-name validated up front в†’ 409 (В§2 step 3). |
| Directus #17657 / #9987 / #23923 | Same-name drop+add NULLs unrelated FK cols; field-drop orphans relations/refs | id-matched ALTER RENAME; `migrateLint` gates relation-orphaning drops; relation target existence is a hard 422; whole-type rewrite sweeps dangling refs in one edit (В§2). |
| Directus #11436 / #25500 | Cache stale after apply; auth froze a startup schema snapshot | Swap is part of the SAME apply call; all subsystems read the mutable `live` cell вЂ” no private frozen snapshot (В§3.1). |
| Directus #25760 / #14687 / #23576 | Diff vs raw introspection в†’ phantom creates/deletes/type-changes | Diff is canonical-JSON `_schema_applied` vs canonical-JSON `next`, vendor-normalized; rebuild uses the same `next` IR (no re-introspect) so re-apply of unchanged schema is a guaranteed no-op (В§3.2). |
| Directus #21486 / #24260 | Diff silently omits a field; 204-vs-object response | `migrate` asserts the change-set; uniform envelope always returned, never branch-on-status (В§1). |
| Directus #24035 | O(catalog) re-introspection doesn't scale | v1 full-side-build is gated under a row-count threshold; incremental (per-type) rebuild is fully specified as the scaling follow-up (В§3.3, R-1). |
| Directus #26697 | One-tx forbids `CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY` | Noted future carve-out for index builds outside the atomic tx (В§4). |
| Directus #24538 | Blind force-overwrite of concurrent edits | Optimistic concurrency on on-disk content hash; no blind force; 412 + re-fetch (В§4). |
| Hasura #8982 | Rename: DDL lands, metadata rejected в†’ broken under both names, no error | File flip (tempв†’rename) + DDL commit + swap are one all-or-nothing unit; boot guard recovers the file-behind window (В§2.1, В§3.4). |
| Hasura #6344 / #3381 | Bad schema crashes boot; fix-it API dies with it | Safe-boot fallback to last-good `_schema_applied`; stay up; `POST /builder/reload` reachable (В§3.5). |
| Hasura #3720 / #1182 | Multi-instance metadata fan-out (NOTIFY machinery) | Single instance в‡’ direct in-process swap, **no** NOTIFY/Redis ChangeBus for schema (В§0.4). |
| Hasura #9648 | Server-strict vs CLI-permissive inconsistency default | ONE strict default everywhere; no permissive client override; `allowDestructive` is the only ack and it's per-op (В§2.3). |
| PostgREST #1688 / #3361 / supabase #42183/#39446 | Serve a schema known behind the DB; PGRST204 on new column; lost NOTIFY в†’ stale forever | Synchronous swap from the just-reconciled IR before 200; writes validate against the just-swapped registry; read-after-write tested (В§3, В§9). |
| PostgREST #2791 / #3778 | Reload-in-progress drops newer signals; dead listener в†’ permanent stale | No signal channel; the single-writer mutex serializes and the swap always reflects the LAST committed state (В§4). |
| Payload #10853 / #14941 / #6595 | drizzle-kit can't tell rename from drop+add; interactive prompt | Machine-decided identity via stable ids; `_schema_applied` updated non-interactively in the migrate tx (В§0.2). |
| Payload #15071 / #4677 / #14580 | enum/ALTER TYPE in one tx; CREATE TYPE already-exists | Enums modeled as CHECK constraints (no `ALTER TYPE`); idempotent diff vs snapshot. |
| Payload #14800 / #16767 | Generated `down()` rollback is wrong/fails | No reverse script вЂ” whole edit in ONE tx with atomic PG rollback (В§2). |
| Payload #15542 | TS compiler dragged into prod runtime | Node в‰Ґ24 runs `.ts` natively; codegen is the only build-time concern and stays out of the hot path; apply path doesn't even re-import (В§3.2). |
| Payload #16254 | Concurrent writes on one pg connection silently lost | Single-writer mutex; DDL on a dedicated `max:1` connection, sequential (В§4). |
| Keystone #2095 / Prisma #19100 | Auto-drop / auto-reset on schema/DB mismatch | Mismatch handled by an implemented boot guard (recover-forward or HALT LOUD); destructive only behind `allowDestructive`; never auto-drop a reverse diff (В§2.1, В§3.4). |
| node #38322 / aral ESM leak | No ESM cache eviction; `?v=` re-import leaks module graph | Apply path rebuilds from the in-memory IR вЂ” **no re-import, no leak**; `?v=` confined to the rare `POST /builder/reload` primitive (В§3.2, В§3.6). |
| Strapi #5038 (reload variant) | Reload returns cached module в†’ silent stale | `loadTypes` takes a per-reload cache-bust token applied to schema.ts + hooks.ts; documented transitive-graph limit (В§3.6). |
| Stripe idempotency | Retried mutation re-applies | `Idempotency-Key` в†’ persisted `_builder_idempotency` table returns stored first result; plus server-side re-mint guard rejects id-less retries of an existing type (В§4). |

---

## 9. Open questions for the maintainer

1. **D&P / i18n toggle on an existing type.** `migrate` throws `MigrationUnsupportedError` for `setTypeOption` today. These are **data migrations**, not flags (Strapi #19141, #25802, #18942): toggling i18n must define what happens to existing rows (copy/share/null per the S1 sync design), and D&P-on must backfill `published_at`. **Decision needed:** ship the toggle as a real, tested migration now, or keep it 422-blocked and require drop+recreate? (The i18n cross-locale-overwrite class demands a test that flipping `localized` never cross-writes existing locale rows.)
2. **Prod Builder default.** В§5 recommends default-off-but-enableable. Confirm: is enabling the prod Builder a supported, documented capability, or strictly an escape hatch?
3. **Incremental vs full rebuild for v1.** В§3.3 ships full side-build for v1 under a row-count threshold and fully specifies the incremental dispatch (createв†’registerDetached, updateв†’replaceType/rebuildType, dropв†’removeType, + relation/inverse targets). Given the engine **already** has those per-type primitives and the be-22 memos cite 2M-row workloads, **should incremental ship from day one** rather than as a follow-up? (See Risk R-1.)
4. **Version token scheme.** В§4 commits to a **content hash of the on-disk catalog** (it is what moves when files move; survives the files-as-truth invariant and `POST /builder/reload`). Confirm the serialize-per-GET cost is acceptable vs a cheaper counter (rejected because it doesn't advance on out-of-band file edits).
5. **Legacy cutover stance.** Translate-and-forward the old `/content-types/*` mutations during a deprecation window, or hard **410 Gone** immediately? Depends on whether any external SDK consumer already drives the granular routes.
6. **`lock_timeout` value + retry policy.** Lower from 5s toward <2s with bounded backoff (В§4) вЂ” confirm the retry count / total budget acceptable for the admin UX, vs failing fast at 409 on the first lock contention.
7. **Component-type Builder parity.** This doc cores the content-type route; component-types (`/component-types/*`) need the identical files-first treatment. Same slice, or separate?
8. **Idempotency-key storage.** В§4 commits to a persisted `_builder_idempotency` table (survives restart, single-instance). Confirm the **TTL** (how long replay protection is retained) and whether the table is pruned by a job or lazily on read.

---

## 10. Risks & mitigations (medium/low items, tracked but not blocking)

| # | Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| R-1 | **Full-catalog rebuild cost on a big-data single instance.** v1 re-streams + re-indexes every type's rows on every edit (`buildEngine`, engine.loader.ts:178). On 2M-row workloads this is seconds-to-minutes of CPU/PG load while the apply PUT holds the mutex and the read pool sees a full re-scan вЂ” turning a sub-second admin edit into a multi-minute one that pressures live reads. | medium | Strongly prefer shipping **incremental from day one** (building blocks exist: `replaceType`/`rebuildType` re-stream one type + its relation targets; dispatch in В§3.3). If full-rebuild ships first, **gate it behind a row-count threshold** and **document the live-read pressure**. Open question #3. |
| R-2 | **Double catalog read under the lock.** Route reads `_schema_applied` for version/lint; `migrate()` re-reads it internally (migrate.ts:394). Harmless under the held mutex (one consistent snapshot) but redundant work. | medium | Read once under the lock, pass `next` through; treat the migrate-internal re-read as a later cleanup (В§4). |
| R-3 | **Advisory lock is intra-tx only.** It cannot serialize the file write that precedes `migrate()`. | medium | The app-level mutex (held from the first catalog read, В§4) is the real serializer; the advisory lock is retained as defense-in-depth against a stray second process / the dev watcher. |
| R-4 | **Version token / files-as-truth coherence.** A snapshot-derived version would not advance on an out-of-band file edit, letting a stale PUT slip the 412. | medium | Version = on-disk content hash, recomputed on GET, apply, **and reload** (В§4, open question #4). |
| R-5 | **Idempotency storage durability.** In-memory key storage is lost on the crash/restart the key exists to survive; and id-less retries still re-mint. | medium | Persisted `_builder_idempotency` table + server-side re-mint guard (reject id-less PUT against an existing type) + explicit SPA "resend the `schema` envelope" contract (В§4). |
| R-6 | **Reload transitive-graph staleness.** `?v=` on reload refreshes only `schema.ts`/`hooks.ts`; shared helpers / `@conti/core` are not re-imported. | low | Document that `POST /builder/reload` reliably refreshes only schema.ts/hooks.ts; a process restart is the only fully-correct refresh of the transitive graph (В§3.6). |
| R-7 | **Codegen injection surface beyond field names.** `generateSchemaSource` emits relation `target`, `inverse`, enum `values`, component refs as source. `lit()`/JSON.stringify neutralizes string-break, but target/component refs become live identifiers downstream and `inverse`/`field` become field keys; `validateFieldName` is only called for `renameField.to` in migrate today. | low | В§2 step 3 validates **every emitted identifier** (relation.field, relation.inverse, apiId, field.name), makes **target/component existence a hard 422** (not a warning вЂ” a dangling ref throws at swap в†’ 500), and rejects control chars in enum values. |
| R-8 | **Public GET exposes stable ids (the rename key).** An attacker could read ids and forge an id-collision rename. | low | Ids confirmed non-secret for confidentiality; the **PUT** path enforces the model: client ids honored only if present in the addressed type's snapshot, unique within the draft, never re-targetable onto a different field (В§2 step 3, В§6). |
| R-9 | **`CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY` incompatible with the one-tx model.** | low | Out of scope until user indexes exist; noted as a future carve-out outside the atomic tx (В§4, Directus #26697). |

---

## 11. Recommended build order (slices)

1. **S1 вЂ” Mutable `live` cell (prerequisite).** Replace `const current = engine` (uws.adapter.ts:377) with the `live = { engine, registry, hooks }` holder; route all closures through `live.*`. No behavior change yet; unblocks the swap. (В§3.1)
2. **S2 вЂ” Harden `applySchemaEdit` atomicity.** Temp-write в†’ `migrate()` в†’ `fs.rename` on commit; unlink-and-re-raise on throw. (В§2.1)
3. **S3 вЂ” Boot reconciliation guard.** Detect destructive reverse diff (file-behind) в†’ recover-forward from `_schema_applied.schema` or HALT LOUD; encode file-behind vs file-ahead direction. (В§2.1, В§3.4) вЂ” turns the core invariant from prose into code.
4. **S4 вЂ” IR-driven build-then-swap.** Rebuild the off-side Engine/Registry from the `next` IR via `loadFromSchemas`/`Registry.fromSchemas`; validate; atomic pointer swap; discard-on-throw в†’ 500. Full-catalog for v1 (gated by row-count threshold). (В§3.2, В§3.3)
5. **S5 вЂ” `PUT /builder/content-types/:apiId` + uniform envelope.** Wire S2+S4 behind the apply flow (В§2): env gate, auth gate, mutex (from first catalog read), single catalog read, full identifier + id-ownership + target-existence validation, status mapping for the 3 migrate error classes. (В§1, В§2, В§4, В§6)
6. **S6 вЂ” Concurrency hardening.** App-level single-writer mutex; on-disk content-hash version + `If-Match`/412; persisted `_builder_idempotency` + server-side re-mint guard; lower `lock_timeout` + bounded retry; dedicated `max:1` DDL connection. (В§4)
7. **S7 вЂ” `GET` + `POST вЂ¦/preview`.** Read applied catalog + version; dry-run mint+lint+codegen with no write. (В§1)
8. **S8 вЂ” `DELETE /builder/content-types/:apiId`.** Always-destructive drop requiring `allowDestructive` + version. (В§1, В§2.3)
9. **S9 вЂ” `POST /builder/reload` (cache-busted).** `loadTypes` cache-bust token on schema.ts + hooks.ts; bump version; build-then-swap; document transitive-graph limit. (В§3.6)
10. **S10 вЂ” Safe-boot fallback.** Build from last-good `_schema_applied` on boot-import failure; surface via `GET /builder` + logs; keep reload reachable. (В§3.5)
11. **S11 вЂ” Prod gating.** `builder.enabled` flag (default off in prod), FS-writability precheck, 403-loud disabled state. (В§5)
12. **S12 вЂ” Legacy cutover.** Translate-and-forward `/content-types/*` mutations (deprecation window) в†’ later 410; back `GET` reads with the applied catalog; delete meta-write ops / meta-driven `rebuildType` / meta tables from the init migration. (В§7)
13. **S13 (follow-up) вЂ” Incremental rebuild.** Per-type dispatch (createв†’registerDetached, updateв†’replaceType/rebuildType, dropв†’removeType, + relation/inverse targets); gate behind a benchmark. **Promote ahead of S-tail if open question #3 says day-one.** (В§3.3, R-1)
14. **S14 (parallel) вЂ” Component-type Builder parity.** Identical files-first treatment for `/component-types/*`. (В§7, open question #7)
